change_summary: Composite index on audit_log(tenant_id, created_at) to remove the sequential scan on tenant-scoped time queries.
critical_invariants: The tenant-scoped time-range query uses the composite index (index scan, not seq scan).
Migration is reversible and adds no lock beyond a brief concurrent index build.
requirement_closure: Ticket ask: Fix slow tenant-scoped audit-log queries via a composite index (NFR performance).
Implemented: CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY on (tenant_id, created_at); query plan asserted via test; down-migration drops it.
repo_gates: cd backend && npm run build [result=pass; attribution=clean] - tsc clean
cd backend && npm test -- auditLogQuery [result=pass; attribution=clean] - 4 passed

self_review_cycles.findings: column order matches query predicate; EXPLAIN confirms index scan
CONCURRENTLY build avoids long lock; reversible down-migration
p95 query 240ms -> 6ms; single index, negligible write cost
